definitions: United States Forces Korea (USFK) is a sub-unified command of United States Indo-Pacific Command (USINDOPACOM). USFK is the ground component for the combined forces in South Korea.
USFK is headquartered in Camp Humphreys, Pyeongtaek, South Korea.
examples: USFK maintains peace and stability on the Korean Peninsula.
The commander of USFK also commands the United Nations Command (UNC) and the Republic of Korea-U.S. Combined Forces Command (CFC).
